name: Woodford County IDHS — Family Community Resource Center
description: Woodford County IDHS Family Community Resource Center in Peoria is the local benefits intake office for SNAP, Medicaid, TANF cash assistance, and child care subsidies for Illinois residents including justice-involved individuals reestablishing food and health coverage after release from Woodford County Jail or IDOC custody. Staff assist with ABE (Application for Benefits Eligibility) online applications, document verification, and emergency medical or food coverage when release paperwork is incomplete. IDHS accepts SNAP and medical applications from approved IDOC facilities within ten days of release through the Pre-Release Program.
description_es: El Centro de Recursos Comunitarios Familiares IDHS del condado Woodford en Peoria es la oficina local de admisión de beneficios para SNAP, Medicaid, asistencia en efectivo TANF y subsidios de cuidado infantil para residentes de Illinois, incluidas personas con antecedentes penales que restablecen cobertura alimentaria y de salud después de la liberación. El personal ayuda con solicitudes en línea ABE, verificación de documentos y cobertura médica o alimentaria de emergencia. IDHS acepta solicitudes SNAP y médicas desde instalaciones IDOC aprobadas dentro de diez días de la liberación.

name: Greater Chicago Food Depository — Partner Agency Network
description: Greater Chicago Food Depository distributes food through hundreds of partner pantries, soup kitchens, and mobile distributions across Cook County and northern Illinois, helping returning citizens locate emergency food by ZIP code through the online food finder. The network connects justice-involved individuals to SNAP outreach partners and nutrition programs—not cash assistance or housing services.

La red conecta personas con antecedentes penales con aliados de SNAP y programas de nutrición.
eligibility: Open to Illinois residents needing food assistance; partner agency rules vary.

Justice-involved returning citizens can establish medical homes, access MAT, and receive behavioral health counseling after release—not a walk-in crisis line.
